name: Rhubarb Mousse with Strawberry-Gin Sauce
recipeIngredient: 1/4 cup Tanqueray or other juniper-flavored gin (such as Gilbey's or Beefeater)
1/4 cup Lemon marmalade with shreds of lemon rather than chunks
recipeInstructions:
(From "Pacific Northwest Palate," by Susan Bradley.)
1. Sprinkle the brown sugar over the rhubarb and let sit for an hour.
2. Place in a saucepan, cover, and simmer gently until fully tender and quite thick (about 15 minutes).
3. Puree in a processor.
4. Soften the gelatin in the gin for 10 minutes.
5. Combine with the rhubarb mixture and heat to dissolve the gelatin.
6. Set into an ice-water bath (a large bowl filled halfway with ice and a couple of cups of water) until cold to the touch, but do not allow the gelatin to set.
7. With an electric mixer, combine the cream and rhubarb mixture. Whip until fluffy and light; do not overwhip or the cream will break down.
8. Beat the egg whites until soft peaks form. Add the sugar and continue beating until stiff peaks have formed.
9. Carefully fold the egg white mixture into the rhubarb and cream mixture.
10. Turn the mousse into a clear glass, 6-cup, straight-sided souffle dish or into individual 1-cup souffle dishes and refrigerate for 2 hours or more.
11. To make the Strawberry Gin Sauce, puree the strawberries and sugar together in a processor. Strain through a sieve and reserve.
12. Warm the marmalade just to melt.
13. Combine the marmalade, gin, and pureed strawberries, mixing well.
14. Chill thoroughly.
15. Serve each portion of mousse topped or surrounded with sauce; pass the sauce separately.
